entries.title: Calibration of Taiji-2: A Comprehensive Analysis

authors.name: Zhang Wei
authors.affiliation: Institute of Mechanics, Chinese Academy of Sciences

authors.name: Li Ming
authors.affiliation: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

entries.abstract: We present a comprehensive calibration analysis for the Taiji-2 mission, focusing on laser interferometry and drag-free control systems. Our results demonstrate the feasibility of achieving the required sensitivity for detecting gravitational waves in the millihertz frequency band.

entries.title: GPU-Accelerated Parameter Estimation for Space-Based Gravitational Wave Detectors

authors.name: Niu Jianwei
authors.affiliation: Institute of Applied Mathematics, Chinese Academy of Sciences

authors.name: Feng Yikun
authors.affiliation: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

authors.name: Zhao Wen
authors.affiliation: University of Science and Technology of China

entries.abstract: We introduce pespace, a GPU-accelerated framework for parameter estimation of gravitational wave signals detected by space-based observatories. The framework achieves significant speedup compared to traditional CPU-based methods, enabling rapid analysis of massive black hole binary signals.

entries.title: Detection and Characterization of Verification Binaries for Taiji

authors.name: Yu Shenghua
authors.affiliation: National Astronomical Observatories, Chinese Academy of Sciences

entries.abstract: We present a comprehensive study of verification binaries that will serve as calibration sources for the Taiji mission. Our analysis identifies 15 high-confidence systems with well-characterized orbital parameters, providing crucial benchmarks for validating Taiji's detection capabilities.

entries.title: Three-Band Dark-Siren Cosmology with Taiji, LISA, and Ground-Based Detectors

authors.name: Song Yijie
authors.affiliation: School of Physics, Sun Yat-sen University

authors.name: Chen Ziming
authors.affiliation: Kavli Institute for Astronomy and Astrophysics, Peking University

authors.name: Fan Xilong
authors.affiliation: Purple Mountain Observatory, Chinese Academy of Sciences

entries.abstract: We investigate the potential of three-band gravitational wave observations combining Taiji, LISA, and ground-based detectors for dark-siren cosmology. Our analysis demonstrates that this multi-band approach can constrain the Hubble constant to sub-percent precision within a decade of observations.

entries.title: Time-Delay Interferometry for Taiji: Algorithms and Implementation

authors.name: Huang Shuanglin
authors.affiliation: Institute of Mechanics, Chinese Academy of Sciences

authors.name: Gong Xuefei
authors.affiliation: Institute of Mechanics, Chinese Academy of Sciences

entries.abstract: We present optimized time-delay interferometry (TDI) algorithms specifically designed for the Taiji mission configuration. Our implementation accounts for the unique orbital characteristics and arm-length variations of Taiji, achieving laser noise suppression of more than 8 orders of magnitude.
